subtitle: Review recovered architecture claims, seams, and confidence gaps before treating them like approved design intent.

sections.title: Major Architecture Claims and Evidence
sections.label: Recovered Architecture
sections.why: Lead with the recovered architecture claims and their carried-forward visual evidence.

sections.title: Runtime, Boundary, and Dependency Reconciliation
sections.label: Reconciliation
sections.why: Use a matrix when architecture claims need cross-dimension reconciliation.

sections.title: Recovered Components and Seams
sections.label: Recovered Seam
sections.why: Keep recovered components and seams dense and evidence-linked.

sections.title: Confidence Hotspots and Inferred-vs-Observed Distinctions
sections.label: Confidence Gap
sections.why: Keep weakly supported architecture claims explicit.

sections.title: Major Risks and Tradeoffs
sections.label: Risk Review
sections.why: Show design risks without pretending recovered evidence is intent.

sections.title: Decisions Required for Approval
sections.label: Decision Gate
sections.why: State the calls that let downstream work trust the recovered architecture enough to use it.

sections.title: Traceability Map
sections.label: Evidence
sections.why: Tie substantive approval claims to exact canonical evidence.

sections.title: Validator Status
sections.label: Validation
sections.why: Show canonical and approval-view validation status together.

sections.title: Downstream Impact if Approved
sections.label: Impact
sections.why: Clarify what downstream work may now treat as fixed.

glance_cards.label: Recovered seams
glance_cards.hint: Recovered seams or hand-offs under review.

glance_cards.label: Confidence gaps
glance_cards.hint: Low-confidence recovered claims needing extra care.

glance_cards.label: Decision gates
glance_cards.hint: Approvals that let downstream work treat claims as stable enough.

glance_cards.label: Evidence claims
glance_cards.hint: Claims backed by exact source quotes.
